ddls:
CREATE TABLE "thai_examples" (
  "example_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each example */,
  "tone_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tone of the example */,
  "pitch_contour_id" INTEGER /* ID of the pitch contour of the example */,
  "pitch_height_id" INTEGER /* ID of the pitch height of the example */,
  "voice_quality_id" INTEGER /* ID of the voice quality of the example */,
  "example_word" TEXT /* Thai word that demonstrates the tone, pitch contour, pitch height, and voice quality */,
  "meaning" TEXT /* Meaning of the example word */,
  "example_sentence" TEXT /* Example sentence */,
  "example_sentence_translation" TEXT /* Translation of the example sentence */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("example_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "frequency_of_occurrence" (
  "frequency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each frequency entry */,
  "tone_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tone */,
  "pitch_contour_id" INTEGER /* ID of the pitch contour */,
  "pitch_height_id" INTEGER /* ID of the pitch height */,
  "voice_quality_id" INTEGER /* ID of the voice quality */,
  "dialect_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dialect */,
  "context_id" INTEGER /* ID of the linguistic context */,
  "frequency" REAL /* Frequency of occurrence of the tone, pitch contour, pitch height, and voice quality in the dialect and linguistic context */,
  "frequency_percentage" REAL /* Frequency of occurrence in percentage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("frequency_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "researchers" (
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each researcher */,
  "researcher_name" TEXT /* Name of the researcher */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the researcher */,
  "researcher_affiliation" TEXT /* Affiliation of the researcher */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("researcher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "contributions" (
  "contribution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each contribution */,
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the researcher who made the contribution */,
  "contribution_date" TEXT /* Date the contribution was made */,
  "contribution_type" TEXT /* Type of contribution (e.g., data entry, data analysis) */,
  "contribution_type_description" TEXT /* Description of the contribution type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("contribution_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_contributions_researcher_id FOREIGN KEY ("researcher_id") REFERENCES researchers ("researcher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, administrator) */,
  "user_department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who accessed the database */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date the database was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., read, write) */,
  "access_type_description" TEXT /* Description of the access type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "updates" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each update */,
  "table_name" TEXT /* Name of the table updated */,
  "column_name" TEXT /* Name of the column updated */,
  "old_value" TEXT /* Old value of the column */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* New value of the column */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date the update was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id")
)

ID of the voice quality of the example
